title: C6-MT023-BottomDrawerStashShelf

scope.summary:
Goal and expected diff shape:
Add the bottom drawer stash shelf -- a collapsible panel that slides up from the bottom of the shell (above the bottom search rail from MT-022) and presents a horizontally-scrolling card shelf populated with typed items: Agenda, Mail, Lists, Notes. The drawer is right-aligned: its affordance (a small tab/handle) sits in the bottom-right corner and is always visible even when the drawer is collapsed. Clicking the tab toggles the drawer open/closed. When open, the drawer occupies a configurable height (default 220px, resizable by dragging the top edge) and shows a horizontal shelf of item cards.

The drawer is architecturally separate from the bottom search rail (MT-022). The rail (32px strip) is always visible at the very bottom; the drawer slides up from just above the rail. When the drawer is open, it compresses the docked tile area upward -- it does NOT overlap content.

Card types on the shelf (all rendered in the same horizontal row, right-aligned so the most-recently-touched item is leftmost):
- Agenda: shows today's date, the count of items due today from the LoomBlock calendar surface (`GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/views/calendar` filtered to today), and a one-line summary of the next item. Clicking opens the LoomCalendarView pane.
- Mail: placeholder card (no live backend today -- Handshake has no mail backend yet); shows a static "Mail" label and a badge count of 0. Clicking shows a "Coming soon" tooltip. The card IS wired to AccessKit so swarm agents can discover it.
- Lists: shows the count of loom blocks with `content_type = "list"` from the current workspace (`GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/views/table?content_type=list&limit=1` to get count from response.total). Clicking opens a pane with the block collection view filtered to lists.
- Notes: shows the count of loom blocks with `content_type = "note"` (same endpoint pattern). Clicking opens a pane filtered to notes.

The drawer affordance is a narrow vertical tab in the bottom-right corner (outside the normal content flow), always rendered above the search rail. Its label is a stash icon (use a unicode character: `⊢` or `•••`, or a simple `[stash]` text label until icons are added). When collapsed the affordance is the only visible element; when expanded the full drawer panel is visible.

Exact files to create or edit (all under `src/frontend/`):
- CREATE `src/frontend/drawer/mod.rs` -- re-exports `DrawerStashShelf`, `DrawerCard`, `DrawerCardKind`.
- CREATE `src/frontend/drawer/stash_shelf.rs` -- the `DrawerStashShelf` struct with fields: `open: bool`, `height: f32` (default 220.0), `cards: Vec<DrawerCard>`, `drag_resize_start: Option<f32>`. Impl `DrawerStashShelf::new() -> Self` and `DrawerStashShelf::show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, app_state: &mut AppState, ctx: &egui::Context)`. The `show` method: (1) renders the affordance tab as a small `egui::Button` at the bottom-right using `ui.with_layout(egui::Layout::right_to_left(egui::Align::BOTTOM), ...)` inside a `TopBottomPanel`; (2) if `self.open`, renders the shelf panel above the rail using `egui::TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.drawer").exact_height(self.height)` registered BEFORE the rail panel in `update()`; (3) inside the shelf panel, renders `egui::ScrollArea::horizontal()` containing the card row.
- CREATE `src/frontend/drawer/card.rs` -- the `DrawerCard` struct: `kind: DrawerCardKind`, `title: String`, `badge_count: u32`, `subtitle: String`, `loading: bool`, `error: Option<String>`. The `DrawerCardKind` enum: `Agenda`, `Mail`, `Lists`, `Notes`. Impl `DrawerCard::show(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) -> bool` (returns true if the card was clicked). Each card renders as a fixed-width (180px) `egui::Frame` containing: kind icon (unicode char), title, badge count chip, subtitle line.
- EDIT `src/frontend/shell/mod.rs` (the App struct and `update()` method) to: (a) add `drawer: DrawerStashShelf` field; (b) add async-fetch state for card data (badge counts); (c) call `self.drawer.show(ctx, ui, &mut self.app_state)` in `update()` BEFORE the rail panel and BEFORE CentralPanel; (d) handle card click callbacks to open panes.

Card data loading:
- Agenda and Lists/Notes counts are fetched from the real Postgres/EventLedger backend via the existing handshake_core HTTP API. The fetch is a one-shot async task fired when the drawer opens; results are cached in `DrawerCard.badge_count` / `.subtitle` until the drawer is closed and re-opened (or an explicit refresh). Failures set `DrawerCard.error` and the card shows an error state without crashing.
- Use `ehttp::fetch` or `reqwest` (whichever the MT-001/MT-005 IPC bridge established) to call the backend. For Agenda: `GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/calendar` -- parse the response for items with `due_date == today_str`. For Lists/Notes: `GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/table` with query params `content_type=list` (or `note`) and `limit=1`; read `response.total` from the `LoomViewResponse` JSON shape `{"blocks": [...], "total": N}`.
- The workspace_id comes from `app_state.active_workspace_id: Option<String>`.
- If `active_workspace_id` is None, all cards show a "No workspace" state with badge 0.

Panel ordering in `update()` (critical -- wrong order causes egui panic):
1. `self.drawer.show(...)` -- registers `TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.drawer")` (height 220px, only if open) and the affordance button inside it.
2. `self.bottom_rail.show(...)` -- registers `TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.bottom_rail")` (32px, always).
3. `CentralPanel::default().show(...)` -- claims remaining space.

Resize handle: The drawer top edge must be draggable to resize height. Implement by sensing `egui::Sense::drag()` on a 4px-tall invisible strip at the very top of the drawer panel. On drag delta y, update `self.height = (self.height - delta.y).clamp(100.0, 480.0)`.

AccessKit requirements:
- Affordance tab button: id `"hsk.drawer.affordance"`, Role::Button, actions: [Action::Click].
- Drawer panel container: id `"hsk.drawer.shelf"`, Role::Group.
- Each card: id `"hsk.drawer.card.{kind_snake_case}"` (e.g. `"hsk.drawer.card.agenda"`), Role::Button, actions: [Action::Click]. Label: `"{title} ({badge_count})"`.
- Resize handle strip: id `"hsk.drawer.resize_handle"`, Role::Slider (semantically closest for a resize handle), actions: [Action::SetValue].
acceptance_criteria: AC-023-1: The drawer affordance tab is always visible in the bottom-right corner of the shell window whether the drawer is open or collapsed.
AC-023-2: Clicking the affordance tab toggles the drawer between open (220px default height) and collapsed (hidden panel, affordance only).
AC-023-3: When open, the drawer renders a horizontally-scrollable card shelf containing exactly four cards: Agenda, Mail, Lists, Notes in that order.

AC-023-4: Each card shows a title, a badge count (integer), and a one-line subtitle. When data is loading the card shows a loading indicator. When the fetch fails the card shows an error string without crashing the shell.

AC-023-5: The Agenda card fetches today's calendar items from `GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/calendar` against the real Postgres backend when a workspace is active. Badge count equals the number of items due today.
AC-023-6: The Lists card fetches count from `GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/table?content_type=list&limit=1` and displays `response.total`. The Notes card does the same with `content_type=note`.
AC-023-7: The Mail card shows badge 0 and a 'Coming soon' tooltip; no backend call is made for Mail.
AC-023-8: The drawer height is user-resizable by dragging the top edge between 100px and 480px.
AC-023-9: When the drawer is open it does NOT overlap the tile pane area; the docked tile area shrinks upward to accommodate the drawer height.
AC-023-10: All drawer interactive widgets have stable AccessKit ids, correct roles, and at least one action (verified by kittest AccessKit tree dump).
AC-023-11: Panel registration order is correct: drawer bottom panel registered before rail bottom panel before CentralPanel. `cargo build` produces no egui ordering panic.

AC-023-12: Clicking the Agenda, Lists, or Notes card triggers the registered `on_card_click` callback (or equivalent AppState event) to open the corresponding pane view. Mail card click triggers a tooltip, not a navigation.
proof_targets: PROOF-023-1 (unit test): `cargo test -p frontend drawer::card` passes testing: (a) `DrawerCard::show` returns true on simulated click, false otherwise; (b) `DrawerCardKind` Display values are `"Agenda"`, `"Mail"`, `"Lists"`, `"Notes"`.

PROOF-023-2 (kittest / visual): Kittest harness with a mocked `app_state` (active_workspace_id = Some("test-ws"), mocked HTTP responses returning canned JSON). Assert: (a) affordance button rendered; (b) clicking affordance sets `drawer.open = true`; (c) four card widgets rendered with correct labels; (d) clicking the Agenda card fires the on-click handler; (e) clicking Mail card does NOT fire a navigation handler.

PROOF-023-3 (integration, real PG): With a live handshake_core server and a test workspace that has at least one loom block with content_type='list': open the drawer and assert the Lists card badge count >= 1 within 3 seconds. `cargo test -p frontend --test drawer_integration -- --ignored` (ignored by default, enabled in CI with PG env). Output: test passes.

PROOF-023-4 (AccessKit): Kittest AccessKit tree dump asserts: `hsk.drawer.affordance`, `hsk.drawer.shelf`, `hsk.drawer.card.agenda`, `hsk.drawer.card.mail`, `hsk.drawer.card.lists`, `hsk.drawer.card.notes`, `hsk.drawer.resize_handle` all present with non-None NodeIds and correct roles.
PROOF-023-5 (build): `cargo build --bin handshake_native` succeeds with zero errors and no egui panel ordering panic in debug run.
implementation_notes: Panel registration order is the single most critical implementation detail. In `App::update()` call self.drawer.show() first, then self.bottom_rail.show(), then CentralPanel. Any other order causes an egui panic at runtime. Add a comment `// ORDER: drawer -> rail -> central -- do not reorder` above the three calls.

The drawer panel (`TopBottomPanel::bottom("hsk.drawer")`) must only be registered when `self.drawer.open == true`. When collapsed, only the affordance button is rendered -- inside the rail panel or as a standalone widget at the bottom-right of the CentralPanel via `ui.allocate_ui_at_rect`.

Alternatively the affordance can be rendered as a small absolute-positioned button overlaid on the corner using `egui::Area::new("hsk.drawer.affordance_area").anchor(egui::Align2::RIGHT_BOTTOM, egui::vec2(-4.0, -36.0))`. This avoids needing to share the rail panel. Use whichever approach keeps the affordance visible in both open and closed states without conflicting with panel allocations.

The horizontal card shelf uses `egui::ScrollArea::horizontal().show(ui, |ui| { ui.horizontal(|ui| { for card in &self.cards { card.show(ui); } }); })`. Card width is fixed at 180px; use `ui.set_min_width(180.0)` inside the card Frame.

Async data fetching: use a `std::sync::mpsc` channel or `tokio::sync::oneshot` to receive fetch results from a background task. On drawer open, if data is stale, spawn a fetch task via the existing AppState async executor (from MT-005). On each `update()` call, drain the channel and update card state. Do not block the UI thread.

The LoomViewResponse JSON shape from `GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/table` is `{ "blocks": [...], "total": N }`. Parse `total` as `u32`. The calendar view shape is `{ "blocks": [...], "total": N }` with each block having a `due_date` field in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Filter client-side for `due_date == today_str` after the fetch.

Mail card: do NOT add a backend call. Set `badge_count: 0`, `subtitle: "Coming soon"`, `loading: false`. On click, use `egui::show_tooltip_text(ctx, egui::Id::new("hsk.mail.tooltip"), "Mail is not available yet")` instead of navigating.

Cards are right-aligned in the shelf -- this means the shelf's `ScrollArea` scrolls LEFT by default to show the rightmost card first. Implement by reversing the card render order (`self.cards.iter().rev()`) OR by calling `scroll_area.scroll_to_cursor(Some(egui::Align::RIGHT))` on open. Simplest approach: render in reverse order so Agenda is rightmost (but labeled first in the list).

Resize handle: add a 4px `ui.allocate_space(egui::vec2(ui.available_width(), 4.0))` at the top of the drawer panel and call `ui.interact(rect, egui::Id::new("hsk.drawer.resize"), egui::Sense::drag())`. On `response.dragged()`, update `self.height -= response.drag_delta().y`.
The `DrawerCardKind` snake_case names for AccessKit ids: Agenda->`agenda`, Mail->`mail`, Lists->`lists`, Notes->`notes`.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/AiJobsDrawer.tsx -- reference for the open/close FAB+drawer pattern (FAB affordance visible when closed, full aside when open). Port the open/close toggle state and the header structure; do NOT port the AI jobs content.

app/src/components/operator/Ans001TimelineDrawer.tsx -- reference for the drawer-as-aside pattern with a header, refresh, and close button. The horizontal card shelf is new (no React equivalent); design is native-first.

app/src/components/LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx -- reference for how the Agenda card should fetch today's journal/calendar blocks using `openDailyJournal` (backend: `PUT /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/journals/:journal_date`). The Agenda card may use this endpoint instead of the calendar view as a simpler data source for the subtitle line.
app/src/components/LoomCalendarView.tsx -- reference for what the Agenda card links to (the calendar view pane).
app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx -- reference for what the Lists and Notes cards link to (a filtered block collection view).
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/calendar -- fetches calendar blocks; filter client-side for today's date. Backend: `handshake_core src/api/loom.rs` route `query_loom_view` with `view_type=calendar`.

GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/table?content_type=list&limit=1 -- fetches total count of list blocks. Backend: `handshake_core src/api/loom.rs` route `query_loom_view` with `view_type=table`, query params `content_type` and `limit`.
GET /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/views/table?content_type=note&limit=1 -- fetches total count of note blocks. Same backend route.

PUT /workspaces/{wsid}/loom/journals/{today_date} -- optional alternative data source for Agenda subtitle (today's daily journal block). Backend: `handshake_core src/api/loom.rs` route `open_daily_journal`.

hbr_obligations: HBR-VIS: all four cards and the affordance tab must have stable AccessKit ids and roles so swarm agents can discover the drawer state and card counts without screen-reading.

HBR-SWARM: card data (badge counts, subtitles) must be readable from AppState without acquiring UI-thread locks; use a read-safe projection field updated on the UI thread from background fetch results.
HBR-QUIET: the drawer must not steal focus or pop any window when it opens; opening the drawer must be initiated only by explicit user or agent action on the affordance.
HBR-INT: the fetch calls to calendar and table view endpoints must have integration tests (marked `#[ignore]` by default, enabled with a real PG URL env var) per PROOF-023-3.

HBR-MAN: the drawer open/close state and card data loading state must be exposed in the AppState debug surface (from MT-011 or equivalent) so a no-context model can inspect drawer state from outside the UI.

risks: RISK-023-A: Panel ordering panic -- if drawer registers its bottom panel AFTER the rail panel or AFTER CentralPanel, egui panics at runtime. This is the highest probability failure.

RISK-023-B: When the drawer is open the combined panel height (drawer 220px + rail 32px) may exceed the window height on small screens, making the CentralPanel area zero or negative, which crashes egui.
RISK-023-C: The fetch for Agenda/Lists/Notes blocks the UI thread if done synchronously, causing visible frame stalls and HBR-QUIET violation.
RISK-023-D: The LoomViewResponse `total` field is used as badge count but the API may return `null` or omit the field if no blocks exist, causing a parse error.
RISK-023-E: Card click routing conflicts: clicking a card triggers both the `DrawerCard.show() -> bool` return value AND a potential drag-detect on the card Frame, causing double-fire navigation.

RISK-023-F: Right-alignment of the card shelf via reversed render order looks correct visually but causes the AccessKit tree to list cards in reverse order, confusing swarm agents that expect Agenda first.
minimum_controls: CONTROL-023-A: AC-023-11 and PROOF-023-5 both gate on `cargo build` with no ordering panic; coder MUST do a debug run before marking the MT complete. The comment `// ORDER: drawer -> rail -> central` is a required code artifact.

CONTROL-023-B: Clamp drawer height: `self.height = self.height.clamp(100.0, (ctx.available_rect().height() - 32.0 - 40.0).max(100.0))` on every `update()` call to prevent combined height exceeding available space.
CONTROL-023-C: Async fetch is mandated in implementation notes via mpsc channel or tokio oneshot; validator must grep for blocking `std::thread::sleep` or synchronous HTTP calls and reject if found.
CONTROL-023-D: Parse `total` with `.and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0)` so a missing or null field defaults to 0 without error.
CONTROL-023-E: Use `ui.interact(card_rect, id, Sense::click())` exclusively for click detection; do not combine with Sense::drag on the same rect.

CONTROL-023-F: Store cards in logical order (Agenda, Mail, Lists, Notes) in the Vec; for AccessKit tree traversal use the Vec order. For visual right-to-left display, use `egui::Layout::right_to_left(Align::TOP)` rather than reversing the Vec.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds the first reviewer: all 12 acceptance criteria map to real code and live-tree/wire-capture proof; the contract's binds_backend_api (/loom/views/table, /loom/views/calendar, content_type=list, response.total) was independently confirmed FABRICATED against handshake_core, and the coder's documented deviation to the verified surfaces (views/all?content_type=note|view_def, PUT /loom/journals/{today}) is the correct, honest MT-022 handling with no spec-realism escape.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-1: Affordance tab always visible (open or collapsed)
acceptance_summary.evidence: stash_shelf.rs:781 show_affordance renders a RIGHT_BOTTOM-anchored egui::Area Button at fixed NodeId 32, called unconditionally in app.rs:1844 drive_drawer step 5. Live-tree test affordance_present_when_collapsed_and_drawer_nodes_absent (test_stash_shelf.rs:51) asserts the affordance node present while collapsed; open_drawer_shows_seven_nodes_with_correct_roles asserts it present when open.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-2: Clicking affordance toggles open (220px default) / collapsed
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs:1844-1846 flips bottom_drawer_open on affordance click; DRAWER_DEFAULT_HEIGHT=220.0 (stash_shelf.rs:113). Live click-by-label test clicking_affordance_toggles_open_state (test_stash_shelf.rs:204) opens then closes via the real AccessKit labels 'Open stash drawer'/'Close stash drawer'.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-3: Open drawer shows horizontally-scrollable shelf with exactly four cards Agenda/Mail/Lists/Notes in order
acceptance_summary.evidence: show_open_panel (stash_shelf.rs:902) wraps a ScrollArea::horizontal over self.cards, seeded from DrawerCardKind::all() in fixed order (stash_shelf.rs:744). CONTROL-023-F honored: visual right-to-left layout (Align::Min) WITHOUT a Vec reversal, so the AccessKit tree keeps logical order. drawer_debug_state test:276 asserts titles == [Agenda,Mail,Lists,Notes]; fresh_shelf_has_four_cards_in_order_at_default_height:1066 confirms the Vec order.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-4: Each card shows title, badge count, subtitle; loading indicator while loading; error string on failure without crashing
acceptance_summary.evidence: DrawerCard::show (stash_shelf.rs:586-598) renders Loading…/Error:{err}/subtitle branches; apply_result (stash_shelf.rs:441) sets error on Err without panic (apply_err_result_sets_error_without_crashing:1048). fetch_view_count (backend_client.rs:914) defaults missing/null blocks to 0 (CONTROL-023-D), never an error.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-5: Agenda fetches today's calendar items from real Postgres when workspace active
acceptance_summary.evidence: Contract endpoint /loom/views/calendar independently CONFIRMED FABRICATED: parse_view_type (api/loom.rs:3107-3113) accepts only all/unlinked/sorted/pins/favorites. Coder used the contract's own ports_from_react alternative PUT /workspaces/:ws/loom/journals/:journal_date -> open_daily_journal (router api/loom.rs:85-86, handler :502), independently verified as a real PUT route. fetch_agenda (app.rs:2180) fires it against the real backend; today_utc_ymd (app.rs:601) computes the UTC date via the Hinnant civil-from-days algorithm. Honest, disclosed deviation.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-6: Lists/Notes fetch count from views/table?content_type=list/note and display total
acceptance_summary.evidence: Contract surface independently CONFIRMED FABRICATED: LoomBlockContentType (storage/loom.rs:41-56) has NO 'list' variant (valid: note/file/annotated_file/tag_hub/journal/canvas/view_def); LoomViewResponse::All { blocks } (api/loom.rs:3099) has NO 'total' field (count = blocks.len(), loom.rs:3099-3102). Coder mapped Lists->view_def, Notes->note via GET /loom/views/all?content_type=<ct> (backend_client.rs:853-862, DrawerDataKind::content_type :790-798) parsing blocks.len() (fetch_view_count :908-925). Proven on the wire by the non-ignored e2e drawer_count_spawn_sends_real_get_and_parses_blocks_len (backend_client.rs:1610).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-7: Mail shows badge 0 + 'Coming soon' tooltip; no backend call
acceptance_summary.evidence: DrawerCard::new(Mail) seeds badge 0 / 'Coming soon' (stash_shelf.rs:410-418); fetches_data()==false for Mail (stash_shelf.rs:214, only_mail_skips_data_fetch:1018); on_hover_text 'Mail is not available yet' (stash_shelf.rs:614). clicking_mail_card_does_not_navigate (test_stash_shelf.rs:170) asserts tab count unchanged after a live click.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-8: Drawer height user-resizable by dragging top edge between 100px and 480px
acceptance_summary.evidence: show_open_panel (stash_shelf.rs:847-859) senses a 4px Sense::drag strip; height -= drag_delta().y clamped DRAWER_MIN_HEIGHT(100)..DRAWER_MAX_HEIGHT(480). Resize node carries Role::Slider + Action::SetValue + numeric min/max (stash_shelf.rs:879-887); live-tree asserts the Slider role (test_stash_shelf.rs:101).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-9: Open drawer does NOT overlap tile area; tile area shrinks upward
acceptance_summary.evidence: Drawer is a TopBottomPanel::bottom('hsk.drawer') registered in drive_drawer (app.rs:1834) which runs at ui() line 4076 BEFORE CentralPanel (app.rs:4227); egui's panel model carves the panel's space from the central region, never overlaps. clamp_height (stash_shelf.rs:772) prevents the drawer+rail+status from collapsing the CentralPanel (RISK-023-B); clamp_height_keeps_room_for_central_panel:1082 + clamp_height_respects_max_on_a_large_window:1093 prove the clamp.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-10: All drawer interactive widgets have stable AccessKit ids, roles, and >=1 action (kittest tree dump)
acceptance_summary.evidence: 7 fixed nodes (band 32..=38) registered in DECLARED_IDENTITIES (registry.rs:478-505, collision-checked). open_drawer_shows_seven_nodes_with_correct_roles (test_stash_shelf.rs:79) asserts affordance/cards=Button, shelf=Group, resize=Slider via the live AccessKit tree; resize carries Action::SetValue. open_drawer_frame_has_no_unnamed_interactive_nodes:240 passes the MT-025 naming gate with the drawer open.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-11: Panel registration order correct (drawer before rail before central); no egui ordering panic
acceptance_summary.evidence: The contract's literal 'drawer->rail->central' assumed an egui stacking convention OPPOSITE to this codebase; the coder correctly registers the drawer AFTER the rail (drive_search_rail at ui() :4068, drive_drawer at :4076) so it stacks ABOVE it, documented at stash_shelf.rs:32-40 and the '// ORDER:' comment app.rs:1831 (CONTROL-023-A artifact present). open_and_close_many_frames_without_ordering_panic (test_stash_shelf.rs:222) runs the REAL update path across many open/closed frames with no unwind; ground-truth confirms compile+suite green.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC-023-12: Clicking Agenda/Lists/Notes opens the corresponding pane; Mail triggers tooltip not navigation
acceptance_summary.evidence: DrawerEvent::OpenCard routed to open_drawer_card_pane (app.rs:1853-1855, 2192) -> open_content_on_active_pane (Agenda->LoomDailyJournal, Lists->LoomBlock+view_def, Notes->LoomBlock+note), the SAME real pane primitive the palette/menu use — not a fake nav. Mail->MailTooltip is a no-op nav (app.rs:1857-1861). clicking_agenda_card_opens_a_pane (test_stash_shelf.rs:135) asserts a tab is inserted; clicking_mail_card_does_not_navigate asserts the tab count is unchanged.

spec_realism.detail: Grep of the required-behavior path (stash_shelf.rs, backend_client.rs MT-023 region) found NO todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/stub-return reachable from the behavior MT-023 must deliver. The ConvertArtifact disabled item is MT-024 action-menu surface, not an MT-023 required path. Real-resource claim (Postgres-backed Loom view-count + daily-journal HTTP API) is proven NOT mock-only by the non-ignored e2e drawer_count_spawn_sends_real_get_and_parses_blocks_len (backend_client.rs:1610): it binds a real std::net::TcpListener, drives the production fetch_count spawn path (dispatch -> tokio spawn -> reqwest -> parse -> delivery cell), captures the exact wire request line 'GET /workspaces/ws1/loom/views/all?content_type=note HTTP/1.1', and parses blocks.len()=2 from the verified LoomViewResponse::All shape. The #[ignore]'d PROOF-023-3 (test_drawer_integration.rs:25) exercises the SAME client against live PG and is contract-mandated to be ignore-gated (HBR-INT) — it hides no required default behavior, since the off-thread fetch path and the render/click/AccessKit path are both covered by non-ignored tests. The contract's binds_backend_api endpoints (/loom/views/table, /loom/views/calendar, content_type=list, response.total) were INDEPENDENTLY re-verified FABRICATED against handshake_core (api/loom.rs:3107 parse_view_type; storage/loom.rs:41 LoomBlockContentType with no list variant; api/loom.rs:3099 LoomViewResponse::All has no total field; api/loom.rs:85 PUT journals route real); the coder's documented deviation to the real verified surfaces is the correct, honest handling of a stale contract — the MT-022 lesson the operator flagged HIGH-priority. Implementer self-certification (lifecycle PENDING) is NOT a FAIL per operator directive; this review IS the independent certification.

concerns_non_blocking: File layout deviates from the contract's 'EXACT FILES TO CREATE' (src/frontend/drawer/{mod,stash_shelf,card}.rs + src/frontend/shell/mod.rs). Agenda badge semantics differ from the contract's 'count of items due today': the journal endpoint returns a single daily-journal block, so badge=1 if today's journal has a non-empty title else 0 (backend_client.rs:945-952). This is an honest mapping given no calendar/due-date surface exists in the backend; the contract's data source itself was fabricated. Worth noting for a future true 'due-today count' WP if a LoomCalendarView surface is added.
